title: Silky Citrus Peruvian Chicken Broth
description: A fragrant, lowcalorie broth infused with lime, orange, ginger, and cumin, finished sousvide for silky texture and nostalgic Peruvian flavor.
instructions: Heat the water to a rolling boil and dissolve the chicken stock cube, stirring until fully dissolved.
Place the chicken breast fillets in a vacuum‑seal bag along with the lime juice, orange juice, tomato paste, minced ginger, ground cumin, chopped cilantro, salt, and pepper.
Seal the bag using a vacuum sealer or the displacement method.
Set the sous‑vide to 165°F (74°C) and immerse the sealed bag. Cook for 90 minutes.
After cooking, remove the bag from the water bath and let it sit for 5 minutes to cool slightly.
Open the bag, remove the chicken, and shred it with two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the bag to keep it moist.
Seal the bag again and strain the broth through a fine mesh sieve into a saucepan, discarding solids. Reheat the broth gently to 180°F (80°C).
Taste the broth and adjust seasoning if necessary. Serve hot, garnished with extra cilantro if desired. Enjoy!
